name: Alabama

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion to require a doctor's note for any absence, any duration
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time under own policy
sickLeave.recentChanges: Ala. Code § 11-80-16 (2014) preempts local governments from enacting paid leave ordinances

employerNotes.details: No state statute governs doctor's note requirements for private-sector employees. Employer may require a doctor's note for any absence, any duration, under any policy. Common industry practice is to require documentation after 3 consecutive days.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: no public policy, limited good faith, limited implied contract — one of the most employer-friendly jurisdictions
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: Federal FMLA and ADA anti-retaliation protections apply; no state-specific health privacy statute limits note content

telehealth.consentRequirement: Written consent required for Medicaid patients; no specific requirement for private pay

forgery.penalties: 1–10 years imprisonment
forgery.medicalSpecific: § 13A-9-21 covers false applications for medical licenses/benefits (Class C felony)
forgery.likelyCharge: Class C felony under § 13A-9-4

contentAngle: Alabama has no mandatory paid sick leave and employers have full discretion — but your employer cannot fire you for using FMLA rights, and a doctor's note from TeleDirectMD satisfies nearly every employer's 3-day documentation threshold.

name: Arizona

sickLeave.lawName: Fair Wages and Healthy Families Act
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employer CANNOT require documentation for fewer than 3 consecutive workdays. Documentation may NOT require disclosure of specific medical condition.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: 3 consecutive workdays — no note allowed for 1 or 2 days
sickLeave.recentChanges: Effective July 1, 2017; no major recent changes

employerNotes.details: Under A.R.S. § 23-373, employers may NOT require documentation for absences fewer than 3 consecutive days. For 3+ days, 'reasonable documentation' is allowed but may not describe the nature of the health condition.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: Employers may not retaliate for using earned paid sick time; employees need not comply with documentation requests for <3 days

telehealth.consentRequirement: Verbal or written; if verbal, must be documented

forgery.penalties: Presumptive 2.5 years; range 1–3.75 years; fines up to $150,000
forgery.medicalSpecific: No medical-specific statute; § 13-2002 covers all written instruments
forgery.likelyCharge: Class 4 felony

contentAngle: Arizona law says your employer CANNOT ask for a doctor's note for 1 or 2 sick days — and even when documentation is required, they cannot demand to know your diagnosis.

name: California

sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employer CANNOT require a doctor's note when employee uses paid sick leave — regardless of absence length. Zero documentation provision in the law.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — notes can never be required for PSL absences
sickLeave.recentChanges: SB 616 expanded minimum from 24 to 40 hours effective January 1, 2024. Accrual cap increased to 80 hours.

employerNotes.details: Under Cal. Lab. Code § 246, employers may NOT require a doctor's note when an employee uses paid sick leave — regardless of length. California Confidentiality of Medical Information Act (CMIA) further limits employer use of medical information.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, implied contract, good faith
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: Cal. Lab. Code § 246.5(c) prohibits discharge, threat, demotion, or suspension for using accrued sick days

telehealth.consentRequirement: Written or verbal; documented in medical record

forgery.penalties: Misdemeanor: up to 1 year/$1,000. Felony: up to 3 years/$10,000
forgery.likelyCharge: Misdemeanor for employer note; felony if used for financial fraud

contentAngle: California law says your employer CANNOT require a doctor's note when you use your paid sick leave — period. No exceptions, no minimum days.

name: Colorado

sickLeave.accrualRate: 1 hour per 30 hours worked; begins day 1
sickLeave.annualCap: 48 hours; up to 48 hours carries over
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employer CANNOT require documentation for 4 or fewer consecutive workdays — most protective threshold in the nation. Documentation may not require disclosure of specific health/safety details.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: 4 consecutive workdays — no note allowed for absences of 4 days or fewer
sickLeave.recentChanges: Effective January 1, 2022 for all employers. Additional 80 hours PHE leave during declared emergencies.

employerNotes.details: Under HFWA (C.R.S. § 8-13.3-401 et seq.), documentation only permitted for 4+ consecutive days absence. Documentation cannot require disclosure of diagnosis or specific medical detail.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: HFWA includes anti-retaliation provisions

telehealth.consentRequirement: Written consent required before first telehealth visit

forgery.classification: Class 2 misdemeanor (for doctor's note); Class 5 felony for government/financial documents
forgery.penalties: Class 2 misdemeanor: up to 120 days jail/$750 fine. Class 5 felony: 1–3 years/$1K–$100K
forgery.likelyCharge: Class 2 misdemeanor for employer note; Class 5 felony if used to obtain insurance or government benefits

contentAngle: Colorado has the most protective sick leave documentation law in the US — your employer cannot ask for a doctor's note unless you've been out 4 or more consecutive days.

name: Connecticut

sickLeave.lawName: Paid Sick Leave Act (as amended by PA 24-8)
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employers are COMPLETELY PROHIBITED from requiring any documentation for paid sick leave absences. No note can be required for any duration.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — documentation is categorically prohibited for PSL absences
sickLeave.recentChanges: PA 24-8 (effective Jan 1, 2025) eliminated all documentation requirements; employer threshold dropping from 25 to 11 (2026) to 1 (2027).

employerNotes.details: CGS § 31-57r et seq. (as amended by PA 24-8, eff. Jan. 1, 2025) prohibits requiring any documentation for PSL absences — categorical prohibition, no duration minimum.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: Employees may not be discharged, penalized, or threatened for exercising PSL rights

telehealth.consentRequirement: Verbal disclosure and verbal consent at first interaction

forgery.penalties: Up to 6 months jail/$1,000 fine
forgery.likelyCharge: 3rd degree forgery — Class B misdemeanor; 2nd degree if used for prescription fraud or benefits

contentAngle: Connecticut employers are now legally prohibited from asking for any doctor's note when you use your paid sick leave — the law changed in January 2025.

name: Delaware

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: Delaware Healthy Delaware Families Act (PFML) began Jan 1, 2026 — this is Paid Family and Medical Leave, NOT paid sick leave

employerNotes.details: No statewide mandatory sick leave statute. Employer policies govern; common practice is to require documentation after 3 consecutive days.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, implied contract (no covenant of good faith)
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA retaliation protections apply; Delaware PFML covers family/medical leave with medical certification requirements

forgery.penalties: Up to 5 years — uniform approach regardless of instrument type
forgery.likelyCharge: Class E felony

contentAngle: Delaware's corporate workforce is sophisticated but protected only by federal FMLA/ADA — no state paid sick leave shield. A TeleDirectMD note provides fast documentation.

name: Florida

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has complete discretion. Fla. Stat. § 448.077 (2013) preempts all local paid leave ordinances.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation for any absence
sickLeave.recentChanges: Preemption of local ordinances strengthened in 2024

employerNotes.details: No state paid sick leave law. No public policy exception, no covenant of good faith, no implied contract exception to at-will doctrine. Florida is arguably the most employer-friendly at-will state.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: none — strongest at-will state
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: Only federal FMLA/ADA protections apply

telehealth.consentRequirement: No specific consent requirement in statute; standard informed consent applies

forgery.penalties: Up to 5 years prison, 5 years probation, $5,000 fine
forgery.medicalSpecific: F.S. § 831.07 specifically targets forging a physician's, dentist's, or licensed practitioner's name or signature — one of only two states with a standalone statute for doctor's note forgery
forgery.likelyCharge: 3rd degree felony under § 831.07 — up to 5 years

contentAngle: Florida has no mandatory sick leave law, and employers can demand a doctor's note for even a single day — get yours fast from TeleDirectMD.

name: Hawaii

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state PSL restriction. Hawaii has TDI (Temporary Disability Insurance) for 8+ day illnesses. For general sick leave, employer policy governs.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No PSL threshold — common standard is documentation after 3+ consecutive days (policy-based)
sickLeave.recentChanges: Hawaii TDI has been in place since 1969; no recent PSL law changes

employerNotes.canRequire: Restricted (for HFLL leave); Discretion (general sick leave)
employerNotes.details: For Hawaii Family Leave Law (HFLL) leave, employers may require medical certification. For general sick leave under company policy, the common standard is documentation after 3+ consecutive days — policy-based, not statutory.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: HRS § 398 prohibits retaliation for taking family leave

telehealth.consentRequirement: Not specifically required by state law; professional standards apply

forgery.penalties: Up to 5 years imprisonment
forgery.likelyCharge: Class C felony under § 708-852

contentAngle: Hawaii's TDI program helps if you're out for over a week — but for shorter absences, your employer still needs a doctor's note, and TeleDirectMD makes it fast.

name: Idaho

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: Idaho Code § 54-5703 (Virtual Care Access Act) effective July 1, 2023 modernized telehealth

employerNotes.details: No mandatory sick leave statute for private employees. Employer may set any documentation policy. Idaho Code § 67-5333 covers public employees (doctor's note for 5+ day absences).
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA protections apply

telehealth.primaryStatute: Idaho Code § 54-5703 et seq. (Virtual Care Access Act)

forgery.classification: Felony — no grading by document type
forgery.penalties: Up to 14 years in state prison — tied with Michigan for highest statutory maximum in the nation
forgery.likelyCharge: Felony; in practice prosecutorial discretion and plea agreements result in lesser sentences

contentAngle: Idaho has no sick leave law, and employers can require a doctor's note for any absence — TeleDirectMD gets you documented fast.

name: Illinois

sickLeave.lawName: Paid Leave for All Workers Act (PLAWA)
sickLeave.accrualRate: 1 hour per 40 hours worked; begins day 1
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Not applicable — leave may be used for any reason whatsoever; employers may NOT require a reason or documentation
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — no documentation or reason required for up to 40 hours/year
sickLeave.recentChanges: PLAWA effective January 1, 2024. Chicago Paid Leave ordinance effective July 1, 2024 provides up to 80 hours total.

employerNotes.details: PLAWA (820 ILCS 192): employers may NOT request reason for paid leave or require any documentation for up to 40 hrs/year. Chicago/Cook County ordinances follow standard 'no note for <3 days' rule for sick-specific leave.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: PLAWA § 30 expressly prohibits retaliation for requesting or using paid leave

forgery.medicalSpecific: 720 ILCS 5/17-8 (Health Care Benefits Fraud): Class A misdemeanor first offense; Class 4 felony subsequent
forgery.likelyCharge: Class 3 felony under § 17-3

contentAngle: Illinois PLAWA means your employer cannot demand any documentation for up to 40 hours of paid leave per year — the law changed in January 2024.

name: Indiana

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time under own policy
sickLeave.recentChanges: State preempts local employment benefit mandates

employerNotes.details: No statewide mandatory sick leave for private-sector. Employers may require documentation for any absence under their own policy. Indiana law enforcement has arrested individuals specifically for fake doctor's notes submitted to employers.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy (limited), no good faith, no implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA apply; no state-specific restriction on note content

telehealth.consentRequirement: Verbal or electronic signature consent required; documented

forgery.penalties: 6 months–2.5 years/$10,000. Level 5 felony with prior conviction: 1–6 years
forgery.medicalSpecific: Indiana police have specifically arrested people for submitting fake doctor's notes to employers
forgery.likelyCharge: Level 6 felony

contentAngle: Indiana has no paid sick leave law — your employer can require a doctor's note for any absence. Get documented quickly and keep your job.

name: Iowa

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: Iowa preempts local employment benefit mandates

employerNotes.details: No state statute governing doctor's note requirements. Employer discretion governs. Common practice: documentation after 3 consecutive days.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA apply

forgery.penalties: Up to 5 years/$7,500
forgery.likelyCharge: Class D felony

contentAngle: Iowa employers can require documentation for any sick day — TeleDirectMD gets you a doctor's note fast, before your shift the next morning.

name: Kansas

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion, but documentation policies cannot be used as pretext to retaliate for workers' comp claims
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: No recent changes

employerNotes.details: No state PSL mandate. Employers may require documentation under own policy (in writing, applied consistently). Kansas courts have held that documentation policies cannot be used as pretext to retaliate for workers' comp claims.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: K.S.A. § 44-501 prohibits workers' comp retaliation; FMLA/ADA apply

telehealth.consentRequirement: Verbal consent followed by written documentation

forgery.penalties: 7–23 months (criminal history dependent — Level 8 with no prior typically results in probation)
forgery.likelyCharge: Level 8 nonperson felony

contentAngle: Kansas has no sick leave protection — a doctor's note from TeleDirectMD is your fastest path to documentation before your aerospace or production shift.

name: Kentucky

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — common 3-day threshold is policy-based only
sickLeave.recentChanges: No recent changes

employerNotes.details: No state mandatory sick leave law. Employers can require documentation for extended absences; common 3-day threshold is policy-based.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA apply; no state-specific sick-leave note retaliation provision

forgery.penalties: Up to 12 months/$500. Could escalate to Class D felony (1–5 yr) if used for benefits fraud.
forgery.likelyCharge: 3rd degree forgery — Class A misdemeanor; may escalate to 2nd degree

contentAngle: Kentucky has no paid sick leave law — UPS Worldport and Amazon workers in Louisville face some of the strictest attendance requirements in the US. TeleDirectMD provides fast documentation.

name: Louisiana

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion. La. Rev. Stat. Ann. § 23:642 (2012) preempts local PSL laws.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: No recent changes

employerNotes.details: No state mandatory sick leave law. Employer may require documentation under own policy, applied consistently. Louisiana is one of the most employer-friendly states (no public policy, no good faith, no implied contract exceptions to at-will).
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: none — very strong at-will state
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: Federal FMLA/ADA only; no state-specific sick-note retaliation provision

forgery.penalties: Up to $5,000 fine OR up to 10 years at hard labor, or both
forgery.medicalSpecific: No grading by document type; all forgery is a single felony category
forgery.likelyCharge: Felony — up to 10 years

contentAngle: Louisiana has no paid sick leave law and among the weakest employee protections in the US. Your employer has full discretion to require a doctor's note — TeleDirectMD provides one fast.

name: Maine

sickLeave.noteRestriction: Not applicable — leave may be used for any reason; no documentation requirement
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — no documentation required for up to 40 hours/year of any-reason leave
sickLeave.recentChanges: Effective January 1, 2021; covers employers with 10+ employees

employerNotes.details: 26 M.R.S. § 637: for up to 40 hrs/yr of any-reason leave, employer may NOT require documentation. Maine also has Maine FMLA equivalent (26 M.R.S. § 843–847): physician certification permitted for that leave.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: implied contract only (no public policy, no good faith)
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: Maine Earned Employee Leave prohibits retaliation for using leave

forgery.penalties: Up to 1 year jail/$2,000 fine
forgery.medicalSpecific: 17-A MRSA § 707-A specifically criminalizes falsifying a patient's health care record — one of only two states with a statute specifically targeting healthcare document fraud
forgery.likelyCharge: Class D crime under § 707-A

contentAngle: Maine's Earned Paid Leave law means you can use up to 40 hours per year for any reason — no doctor's note required. For absences beyond that, TeleDirectMD provides fast documentation.

name: Maryland

sickLeave.statute: Md. Code, Lab. & Empl. § 3-1301 et seq.
sickLeave.accrualRate: 1 hour per 30 hours worked; up to 40 hours/year; max 64 hours accrual balance
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employer CANNOT require documentation for 2 or fewer consecutive shifts. Documentation may NOT disclose diagnosis or condition.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: More than 2 consecutive shifts — no note for 1 or 2 shifts
sickLeave.recentChanges: Montgomery County has more expansive local ordinance (up to 80 hrs accrual; 56 hrs paid for 5+ employees)

employerNotes.details: MHWFA: documentation only after 3+ consecutive scheduled shifts. Employer expressly prohibited from asking for diagnosis or condition.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: MHWFA prohibits discharge or discrimination for exercising Act rights

telehealth.primaryStatute: MD Insurance Code; Board of Physicians regulations
telehealth.consentRequirement: Providers must inform patients of risks; recordkeeping requirements apply

forgery.penalties: Up to 3 years/$1,000
forgery.medicalSpecific: Health-General § 4-308 (unauthorized access to medical records — misdemeanor)
forgery.likelyCharge: Misdemeanor under § 8-606 for employer note; felony under § 8-607 if used for financial fraud

contentAngle: Maryland's Healthy Working Families Act means your employer can only request a doctor's note after you've missed more than 2 consecutive shifts — and they can never ask for your diagnosis.

name: Michigan

sickLeave.annualCap: 72 hours (11+ employees); 40 hours paid + 32 hours unpaid (≤10 employees)
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employer CANNOT require documentation for 3 or fewer consecutive days. If documentation required for 4+ consecutive days, employer must pay employee's out-of-pocket costs to obtain it.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: 3 consecutive days — no note for 1, 2, or 3 days; employer pays cost if required for 4+
sickLeave.recentChanges: ESTA effective February 21, 2025 — comprehensive overhaul. ALL employers now covered (was 50+). Cap increased to 72 hours (was 40). Employer pays documentation costs.

employerNotes.details: ESTA: no documentation for ≤3 consecutive days; employer pays costs for 4+ day documentation. 15 days given to obtain documentation. Unlimited carryover.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: ESTA prohibits discrimination/discharge for using or attempting to use earned sick time

forgery.penalties: Up to 14 years — tied with Idaho for highest statutory maximum in the nation
forgery.medicalSpecific: MCL § 750.248a (forging signatures); Michigan courts have prosecuted fake doctor's note cases under § 750.248
forgery.likelyCharge: Felony — up to 14 years (actual sentences far lower)

contentAngle: Michigan's new 2025 sick leave law covers nearly every worker in the state — and now your employer must pay the cost of any doctor's note they require.

name: Minnesota

sickLeave.accrualRate: 1 hour per 30 hours worked; 48 hours/year; max 80 hours accrual
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employer CANNOT require documentation for 2 or fewer consecutive scheduled workdays. If documentation required for 3+ days, employee may substitute a written statement if documentation creates unreasonable burden.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: 2 consecutive scheduled workdays — no note for 1 or 2 days
sickLeave.recentChanges: ESST effective January 1, 2024; covers all employers

employerNotes.details: ESST: documentation only after 2+ consecutive days; employee may substitute written self-certification if note is an undue burden. Documentation cannot require specific diagnosis.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: ESST includes anti-retaliation provisions

forgery.penalties: Up to 3 years/$5,000
forgery.likelyCharge: Forgery under § 609.63 — up to 3 years/$5,000

contentAngle: Minnesota's ESST law protects workers from note requirements for short absences — but for 3+ day absences, TeleDirectMD provides fast, affordable documentation.

name: Missouri

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion. Proposition A (voter-approved Nov 2024) briefly mandated PSL but was repealed by legislature Aug 28, 2025.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: CRITICAL: Proposition A (voter-approved Nov 5, 2024) mandated PSL from May 1–Aug 28, 2025. Legislature passed HB 567; Governor signed July 10, 2025; repeal effective August 28, 2025. Missouri currently has NO PSL law.

employerNotes.details: No state paid sick leave law (repealed Aug 2025). Employer may require documentation under own policy. State preemption of local ordinances (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 290.528, 2015) remains in effect.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy (no good faith, no implied contract)
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA federal protections only

telehealth.consentRequirement: Written consent required specifically for store-and-forward

forgery.penalties: Up to 7 years/$10,000
forgery.medicalSpecific: No grading by document type; all forgery is Class D
forgery.likelyCharge: Class D felony — up to 7 years

contentAngle: Missouri's voters approved paid sick leave — but the legislature repealed it. Your employer now has full discretion to require a doctor's note for any absence. TeleDirectMD gets you documented fast.

name: Montana

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No PSL restriction. However, Montana uniquely requires employers to have written, communicated, consistently-applied policies before terminating post-probation employees.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation, but must follow written policy
sickLeave.recentChanges: No recent changes

employerNotes.details: Montana is the ONLY U.S. state that is NOT at-will after the probationary period. Under WDEA (Mont. Code Ann. §§ 39-2-901 et seq.), employers must have 'good cause' to terminate employees who have completed probation. Documentation policies must be written, clearly communicated, and consistently applied.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: NOT at-will after probationary period — Wrongful Discharge from Employment Act (WDEA) requires good cause for termination
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: WDEA provides damages of up to 4 years' lost wages; 1-year statute of limitations

forgery.penalties: Typically 2–10 years/$50,000; value of benefit obtained drives severity
forgery.likelyCharge: Felony — sentence based on value of benefit obtained

contentAngle: Montana is the only state where your employer must have a real 'good cause' to fire you after your probationary period — but that still means having proper sick leave documentation matters. TeleDirectMD provides it fast.

name: Nebraska

sickLeave.accrualRate: 1 hour per 30 hours worked; use begins after 80 hours of employment
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Documentation only permitted for absences of more than 3 consecutive days. Acceptable: healthcare provider statement OR written self-certification from employee.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: More than 3 consecutive days — no note for 3 days or fewer; self-certification is an express alternative
sickLeave.recentChanges: New law effective October 1, 2025 (Initiative 436, voter ballot Nov 2024)

employerNotes.details: Nebraska HFWA: documentation permitted only for absences of more than 3 consecutive days. Self-certification is an express alternative if obtaining provider note creates unreasonable burden or expense.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: good faith, implied contract (no public policy exception)
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: HFWA prohibits retaliation for using or requesting sick leave

telehealth.consentRequirement: Written consent required before initial telehealth consultation

forgery.classification: Class II misdemeanor (for employer note with no financial gain)
forgery.penalties: Up to 6 months/$1,000 for Class II misdemeanor; escalates by value of benefit obtained
forgery.medicalSpecific: Neb. Rev. Stat. § 71-3,175 (Filing false medical documents — separate offense)
forgery.likelyCharge: Class II misdemeanor for employer note with no financial gain; escalates based on benefit

contentAngle: Nebraska's new sick leave law just took effect — here's what workers at Omaha's meatpacking plants and financial services employers need to know about their documentation rights.

name: Nevada

sickLeave.accrualRate: ~1 hour per 52 hours worked (~40 hours/year for full-time)
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Not applicable — leave for any reason; no reason or documentation required. Employer may not ask reason.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — no documentation or reason required for mandatory paid leave
sickLeave.recentChanges: Effective January 1, 2020; covers private employers with 50+ employees

employerNotes.details: Employers may NOT require documentation or reason for leave under the mandatory paid leave law. For leave policies beyond this mandate, employer discretion applies.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: NRS 608.0197 prohibits retaliation for using or attempting to exercise paid leave rights

telehealth.consentRequirement: No additional consent beyond standard care required

forgery.likelyCharge: Category D felony — 1–4 years

contentAngle: Nevada workers at companies with 50+ employees can use their paid leave for any reason — no doctor's note required. But for smaller employers or leave beyond your annual hours, TeleDirectMD gets you documented fast.

name: New Hampshire

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: NH PFML (RSA 282-B) is a voluntary, insurance-based opt-in program — not an employer mandate

employerNotes.details: No state mandatory sick leave law. Employer sets own documentation requirements.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA apply

forgery.classification: Class B misdemeanor (for other written instruments)
forgery.penalties: Up to 1 year/$1,200 — one of the least severe criminal outcomes in the nation for this offense
forgery.likelyCharge: Class B misdemeanor — unless used for insurance fraud (then Class B felony up to 7 years)

contentAngle: New Hampshire has no sick leave law — your employer can require a doctor's note for any absence. TeleDirectMD provides documentation without the wait.

name: New Jersey

sickLeave.lawName: New Jersey Earned Sick Leave Law
sickLeave.accrualRate: 1 hour per 30 hours worked; frontloading of 40 hours permitted
sickLeave.annualCap: 40 hours; carryover permitted (use capped at 40 hrs/yr)
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employer CANNOT require documentation for fewer than 3 consecutive days (1 or 2 days: no note allowed). Documentation may not require disclosure of specific medical reason.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: 3 consecutive days — no note allowed for 1 or 2 days
sickLeave.recentChanges: Feb. 2026 court decision clarified PTO policies cannot require note for absences under 3 days

employerNotes.details: NJ ESL: documentation only for 3+ consecutive days; employer may ask about reason for leave (whether covered) but cannot require documentation below threshold. Documentation may not explain 'nature' of health condition.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: ESL prohibits penalizing, disciplining, or discharging for using sick leave

telehealth.consentRequirement: Not explicitly required; standard of care applies

forgery.penalties: Up to 18 months/$10,000
forgery.medicalSpecific: NJSA § 2C:21-4 (Falsifying or Tampering with Records — 4th degree)
forgery.likelyCharge: 4th degree crime; 3rd degree if used in court proceedings or insurance claim

contentAngle: New Jersey law says your employer cannot ask for a doctor's note for 1 or 2 sick days. If you've been out 3 or more days, TeleDirectMD provides documentation fast — and your employer cannot ask what your diagnosis is.

name: North Carolina

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion. State preempts local PSL ordinances (N.C. Gen. Stat. § 95-25.1, 2016).
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: NC Medical Board Position Statement 5.1.4 (March 2024) explicitly authorized async telehealth for relationship formation

employerNotes.details: No state mandatory sick leave law. Federal FMLA/ADA are the primary protections. NC REDA protects against retaliation for workers' comp and wage claims — not sick leave generally.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith (no implied contract)
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: NC REDA (N.C. Gen. Stat. § 95-241) protects against retaliation for workers' comp claims; FMLA/ADA apply

forgery.penalties: ~15 months (lowest felony class)
forgery.likelyCharge: Class I felony — still a felony despite being lowest classification

contentAngle: North Carolina has no state sick leave law, so employers can require documentation for any absence — TeleDirectMD provides a licensed doctor's note fast.

name: North Dakota

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has full discretion. State preempts local PSL ordinances (N.D. Cent. Code § 34-01-15.1, 2021).
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation at any time
sickLeave.recentChanges: No recent changes

employerNotes.details: No state mandatory sick leave law. Employers may require documentation for any absence.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA apply

forgery.penalties: Up to 5 years/$10,000
forgery.likelyCharge: Class C felony

name: Ohio

name: Pennsylvania

name: Texas

sickLeave.noteRestriction: No state restriction — employer has complete discretion. Austin, Dallas, and San Antonio local ordinances passed 2018–2019 are ALL permanently blocked by court rulings.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: No threshold — employer may require documentation for any absence
sickLeave.recentChanges: HB 4156 (effective September 1, 2025) restructured forgery penalty ladder. All local PSL ordinances remain blocked.

employerNotes.details: No state sick leave statute. Employer may require a note for any absence, any duration. Employer cannot demand detailed medical information beyond confirming the absence (ADA-compliant confidentiality applies).

employerNotes.antiRetaliation: FMLA/ADA federal protections only; no state-specific sick-note retaliation provision

name: Washington

sickLeave.statute: RCW 49.46.200 et seq.; WAC 296-128-600 et seq.
sickLeave.accrualRate: 1 hour per 40 hours worked; no accrual cap
sickLeave.annualCap: No annual cap; unused leave up to 40 hours carries over
sickLeave.noteRestriction: Employer CANNOT require documentation for 3 or fewer workdays. Employer must have a written policy to require documentation at all. Documentation may NOT explain nature of condition. Self-certification acceptable if note is a burden. At least 10 days given to provide verification.
sickLeave.noteThreshold: 3 workdays — no note for 1, 2, or 3 days; written employer policy required; self-certification available
sickLeave.recentChanges: Jan 1, 2025: family member definition expanded to include any person regularly residing in employee's home

employerNotes.details: RCW 49.46.210: documentation only after 3+ days; written policy required; nature of condition prohibited from disclosure; self-certification acceptable if note is a burden.
employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: RCW 49.46.210 prohibits retaliation for using paid sick leave; employer may not discipline for any absence where leave was used for authorized purpose

forgery.penalties: Up to 5 years/$10,000
forgery.likelyCharge: Class C felony — up to 5 years

contentAngle: Washington state law requires your employer to have a written policy before they can even ask for a doctor's note — and if providing one would be a burden, you can submit a self-certification instead.

name: Wisconsin

employerNotes.atWillExceptions: public policy, good faith, implied contract
employerNotes.antiRetaliation: WFMLA prohibits discharge or threat for exercising rights

forgery.penalties: Up to 6 years/$10,000 — one of the more severe consequences for fake doctor's notes
